description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped rocks, each with a rugged, angular form and a weathered surface. The rocks display a mottled coloration, primarily gray with patches of greenish moss and hints of brown, suggesting exposure to natural elements. The largest rock has a broad, flat face with jagged edges and a protruding section, while the two smaller rocks are more rounded and compact. The surfaces are rough and uneven, with visible crevices and natural fractures. The rocks appear solid and dense, typical of granite or similar hard stone. Their arrangement and size suggest they could serve as natural obstacles or decorative elements in an urban landscape.
description: A group of three rugged, weathered rocks with gray, green, and brown tones, featuring rough, angular surfaces and natural fractures.
description_view_0: The largest rock is most prominent, showing its broad, flat face and jagged edges, with the two smaller rocks spaced apart.
description_view_1: The rocks are seen edge-on, emphasizing their vertical profiles and the angularity of their forms.
description_view_2: The largest rock is viewed from the side, highlighting its thickness and the rounded nature of the smaller rocks.
description_view_3: The rocks are seen from above, revealing their spatial arrangement and the irregular outlines of each piece.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
